role: A shared React hook that manages chip-triggered popover open/close state and viewport-right-edge collision anchoring for optics chip menus.
capabilities: Flips menu anchor from left-aligned to right-aligned when menu would overflow viewport
Exposes wrapper ref and modifier class for styling
substance_why: It fully implements stateful toggle/close behavior plus a real collision-detection rule using getBoundingClientRect and window.innerWidth.
performance_risks: Synchronous getBoundingClientRect read on every open can force layout
setMenuClass called inside the setOpen updater triggers an additional render cycle
No debouncing or throttling for repeated toggles
reuse_why: The collision-anchoring concept and measurement logic are worth keeping, but the hook needs accessibility, outside-click/Escape handling, and safer render scheduling.
decomposition_hints: Extract viewport collision math into a pure positioning utility
Split generic open/close state management from collision-aware positioning
Move global event handling (outside click, Escape, resize) into a separate interaction hook
